sentence: Subjective:

The patient, a 45-year-old female, reports being okay, but experiencing shortness of breath, which started over the weekend, stating she was previously active and could walk her dog for a mile but now can only walk down the street without getting short of breath.
She denies fever and has a dry cough but mentions that swallowing hurts, particularly in the chest area.
She reports being able to drink liquids and take Ensure, but has decreased her intake due to pain.
She is emotionally supported by her husband and daughter.
She reports being a little nauseated and tired from chemotherapy but took Zofran and got rest, which helped.
She has a Dobrahan dog.
Past medical history:
The patient has stage three non-small cell lung cancer, diagnosed recently, and is undergoing neo-adjuvant chemotherapy with cisplatin and etoposide.
Review of systems:
The patient reports shortness of breath, dry cough, pain when swallowing, and decreased ability to drink liquids due to pain.
Current medications:
I prescribed Zofran as needed for nausea.
Objective:
Vital signs:
The patient's pulse ox is normal, and her other vitals are within normal limits.
Physical exam:
The patient has crackles in her lungs bilaterally, erythema on the anterior aspect of her chest on the left side, and no lower extremity edema in her legs.
The cervical lymphadenopathy and subclavicular adenopathy are not appreciated.
Test Results:
The patient had a chest X-ray, which showed mild radiation pneumonitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has stage three non-small cell lung cancer; radiation pneumonitis, confirmed; and odynophagia, suspected.
Plan:
I prescribed prednisone 40 milligrams daily for five days to reduce inflammation in the lungs and help with breathing.
I also prescribed lidocaine spray to be used four times a day to alleviate pain when swallowing.
Follow-up:
The patient will see me back in two weeks.
